local-gov-disaster-strategies:
Share of local governments that implemented local disaster risk reduction strategies in line with national strategies

This choropleth world map shows values for 2024 using a color ramp that moves from pale yellow for 0% through teal and medium blue to dark blue for 80% and above, with diagonal hatching marking no data. The darkest blue band covers the United States, Canada, Russia, China, India, Indonesia, most of Western and Eastern Europe, and much of South America including Brazil and Argentina. Egypt, the Nordic countries, and several other European nations also fall in this top band. Australia is colored in a teal shade corresponding to the mid portion of the scale, and South Africa sits in a similar teal band. A few African nations including Madagascar and Mozambique appear in teal shades, while Nigeria, Kenya, and Botswana display lighter green-yellow tones sitting in the lower portion of the scale. A handful of countries including Saudi Arabia and several in West Africa appear in the palest yellow shade near the bottom of the range. Diagonal hatching spans Greenland, a broad swath of Central and North Africa, portions of the Middle East, parts of Central Asia, and a group of countries across the southern half of South America shown in white. The overall pattern places high shares across North America, Europe, Russia, China, and Indonesia, with mid-range values across Australia and southern Africa, lower values in a cluster of North African and Middle Eastern nations, and broad gaps in the data across Central Africa and the Middle East.
share-electricity-source-facet:
Share of electricity production by source

This figure contains six small line charts covering 1985 to around 2025 for the United Kingdom in orange, the United States in brown, France in blue, and Sweden in green. In the Coal panel, the United Kingdom starts above 60% and the United States near 55%; both hold high levels through the mid-1990s and then decline, with the United Kingdom falling close to zero by the early 2020s and the United States trending down to around 15%. France and Sweden remain near the bottom throughout. In the Gas panel, the United Kingdom rises from near zero in the early 1990s to above 40% by the early 2000s and stays in the 30% to 40% range, while the United States climbs gradually to above 40% by the end of the series; France and Sweden stay near the bottom. In Nuclear, France remains the highest, moving between 60% and 75%, while Sweden sits between roughly 35% and 50%, and the United Kingdom and United States track between 10% and 25%. In Hydropower, Sweden fluctuates between 40% and 50%, the United Kingdom and France sit between 5% and 20%, and the United States hovers near 10%. The Solar panel stays near zero until about 2010, then rises, with the United Kingdom reaching around 6% by the end of the series. In Wind, values begin near zero and climb after 2005, with the United Kingdom reaching above 25% and Sweden approaching 25%.
reported-cases-of-measles:
Reported cases of measles

This choropleth world map presents 2023 values on a scale that runs from pale cream at zero through deepening orange and red tones up to a dark maroon shade above 10,000, with thresholds at 30, 100, 300, 1,000, 3,000, 10,000, and 30,000 cases, and diagonal hatching for no data. The deepest maroon band is occupied by India and the Democratic Republic of the Congo, with Yemen also sitting in a dark red shade. Russia, Indonesia, Kazakhstan, Ethiopia, and several countries in the Middle East fall into the medium to dark red bands in the thousands range. A broad swath of Sub-Saharan Africa including Nigeria, Sudan, Kenya, Tanzania, and Angola sits in orange tones corresponding to the mid range, while the Philippines, Malaysia, and several Southeast Asian nations also display orange shades. Much of Europe, North Africa, and parts of South America are in light orange to pale cream, with Brazil, Argentina, Chile, and Peru in the palest shades. Australia and New Zealand also fall in the lowest visible bands. Diagonal hatching covers the United States, Canada, Greenland, France, and a few other territories. The visible distribution places the highest reported counts across South Asia, parts of Central Africa, the Arabian Peninsula, and Russia, with low values across the Americas, most of Western Europe, and Oceania, and missing data for North America and several scattered territories.
neonatal-mortality-ihme:
Neonatal mortality rate

The share of newborns who die before reaching 28 days of age.

This choropleth world map shows 2019 values on a scale that runs from pale cream at 0% through deepening orange shades to a dark red band at 10% to 25%, with thresholds at 0.5%, 1%, 2.5%, 5%, and 10%. The deepest red tones cluster across a band of Sub-Saharan Africa, with countries such as Chad, the Central African Republic, Somalia, and South Sudan appearing in the darkest shade. Surrounding nations across West Africa and the Sahel, along with parts of East Africa, display dark orange tones corresponding to the 5% to 10% range. South Asia, including Pakistan and Afghanistan, shows mid-range orange shades falling in the 2.5% to 5% band, while India sits in a similar mid orange tone. Much of South America, Central America, North Africa, and Southeast Asia appears in lighter orange shades in the 1% to 2.5% range. The United States, Canada, Europe, Russia, Australia, New Zealand, and Japan are rendered in the palest cream tones at the lowest end of the scale. No country on the map displays the diagonal hatched pattern for missing data within the visible land areas. The visible distribution concentrates the highest shares across a broad band of Sub-Saharan Africa and parts of South Asia, with the lowest shares spread across North America, Europe, East Asia, and Oceania, and intermediate values across Latin America and parts of Southeast Asia.
joint-adoptions-same-sex-partners-equald:
Joint adoptions by same-sex partners

Same-sex partners can adopt a child legally, with both parents being recognized as such.

This choropleth world map presents 2025 values using a categorical legend with eight classes: Legal in blue, Married couples only in green, Second parent adoption only in pale yellow-green, Varies by region in pale yellow, Individual only in tan, Ambiguous in orange, Illegal in red, and No data in diagonal hatching. Blue covers the United States, Canada, most of Western Europe, Australia, New Zealand, Argentina, Brazil, Colombia, Chile, and South Africa. Red spans Russia, much of the Middle East and Central Asia, a broad band of Sub-Saharan Africa, Indonesia, and several countries in Southeast Asia. Tan covers a large portion of Asia including China, India, Mongolia, and parts of Eastern Europe and North Africa. Orange marks a small number of countries including Peru and a few scattered territories. A few countries in Europe appear in the pale yellow-green second parent adoption class and in the green married couples only class. Diagonal hatching appears across several territories in Central Africa, parts of the Middle East, and a few smaller states. The visible distribution places legal recognition across the Americas, Western Europe, and Oceania, shows widespread illegal status across Russia, the Middle East, and much of Africa, and shows a large block of tan across Asia indicating individual-only pathways, with scattered intermediate categories and patches of missing data.
local-govts-risk-reduction:
Share of local governments with disaster risk reduction strategies

Share of local governments that have adopted and implemented local disaster risk reduction strategies in line with the national strategy.

This choropleth world map displays 2024 values on a scale that runs from pale yellow at 0% through light and medium greens to dark green at 90%, with bands at every 10% interval and diagonal hatching for no data. The darkest green band covers the United States, Canada, Russia, China, India, Indonesia, Japan, the Nordic countries, and much of Western and Eastern Europe. Brazil, Argentina, and several other South American countries also sit in this top band. Australia appears in a medium green shade in the middle of the scale. South Africa and a few other southern African nations display mid-range green tones, while a cluster of countries including Botswana and several in the Sahel appear in lighter yellow-green shades toward the lower end. A handful of countries across North Africa and parts of the Middle East appear in the palest yellow. Diagonal hatching covers Greenland, a broad swath of Central Africa, portions of the Middle East and Central Asia, and a group of countries across the southern half of South America shown in white. The visible distribution concentrates the highest shares across North America, Europe, Russia, China, India, and much of South America, with medium values across Australia and southern Africa, lower values in a cluster of North African and Sahel nations, and extensive gaps in data coverage across Central Africa and the Middle East.
international-trips-for-personal-reasons:
Arrivals of tourists from abroad for personal purposes

Number of international trips where the main purpose is personal (leisure, visiting friends and relatives, health, etc.) and the visitor stays overnight.

This choropleth world map presents 2024 values on a blue color ramp running from a pale near-white shade at zero to a deep dark blue band above 10 million, with thresholds at 500,000, 1 million, 5 million, and 10 million, and diagonal hatching for no data. The darkest blue band is occupied by the United States, Mexico, Turkey, Japan, and Saudi Arabia. A slightly lighter dark blue covers Canada, Spain, Italy, Greece, Poland, and a few additional European states, along with Australia. Medium blue shades mark Germany, the United Kingdom, several other Western European countries, India, Indonesia, South Africa, and Argentina. Lighter blue tones extend across portions of South America including Brazil, Chile, Peru, and Colombia, as well as parts of Southeast Asia. Pale shades near the bottom of the scale cover many smaller countries across Central America, West Africa, and Central Asia. Diagonal hatching spans Russia, China, Greenland, a broad swath of the African continent including much of Central, East, and North Africa, and a scattering of other territories. The visible pattern concentrates the highest arrival volumes in North America, Southern Europe, parts of the Middle East, and East Asia, with medium values across Western Europe, South Asia, and Oceania, lower values across much of Latin America, and extensive gaps in reported figures across Russia, China, and much of Africa.
